title: How far Will you go?
summary: “And Terah took Abram his son, and Lot the son of Haran his son's son, and Sarai his daughter in law, his son Abram’s wife; and they went forth with them from Ur of the Chaldees, to go into the land of Canaan; and they came unto Haran, and dwelt there.” Genesis 11:31 For some reason, Terah, the father of Abraham, decided to go to the Land of Canaan. This was before the Lord gave the call to Abraham to go to the Land of Canaan. It is important that I rectify this misunderstanding. I have heard many Christians state that Abram heard the call of God in Ur of the Chaldees, but this is not true. Abram the call of God to go to the Land of Canaan in the Haran, Syria, where his father, Terah, had settled. It was actually Terha his father who left Ur for the Land of Canaan. “So Abram departed, as the LORD had spoken unto him; and Lot went with him: and Abram was seventy and five years old when he departed out of Haran” (Genesis 12:4). Could it be that Terah, like Abraham, was a God-fearing man who knew that his faith in the One God of Israel would not survive the idolatry of Ur of the Chaldees? We may never know the reason for his decision to up sticks and move towards the Land of Canaan, but we read that he did make the decision and took with him Abram, Sarai and Lot.
